message:
refactor(fwc): Pivot from scaffold simulation to real execution primitives

Transitions the fwc CLI from scaffold-backed simulation to honest execution
semantics, clearly separating real primitives from unimplemented verbs.

Intent compiler (intent.rs):
- Rename SCAFFOLD_NOTICE to EXECUTION_NOTICE, updating all assumption text
  from "scaffold remains honest" to "real primitives will execute"
- Add unsupported_reasons field to CompiledIntent for tracking why an intent
  cannot be executed
- Move enable/disable/start/stop/restart/logs to new "unsupported" family
  with build_unsupported_plan() that generates helpful guidance instead of
  fabricating execution promises

Command surface (main.rs):
- Remove Enable, Disable, Start, Stop, Restart, Logs, Events command
  variants and their arg structs — these are not real primitives yet
- Implement real install_dispatch() and update_dispatch() with source path,
  connectors_file, and version constraint parameters
- Update CLI help text and examples to reflect real execution semantics

Catalog (catalog.rs):
- Add 7 new evidence commands: supply-chain, audit, manifest, net, trace,
  policy, package with full contract definitions
- Expand execution family with cancel, export-tools, serve-mcp, suggest,
  template, validate, history, pipe, map, batch-file
- Simplify lifecycle family to: status, install, update, pin, unpin, rollout

Recovery (recovery.rs):
- Remove aliases and typo corrections for deleted commands
- Update dangerous-shape guidance to recommend status/unpin/update
- Clean up is_mutating_command and is_readonly_command checks

Tests updated across serve_mcp.rs and workflow.rs to reflect new semantics.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
